body.title: Rack devices: /ticket command in chat window
body.result: /ticket command wired in chat POST; _handle_chat_slash_commands() reusable; 4 tests
body.description:
When a human is working with a rack device (via its web chat window) and encounters an issue, there is no easy feedback path — they have to go to the queue manually. Every device chat window should accept /ticket <description> as a command that files the ticket to cc_queue.py and responds with a confirmation (even if the response is just 'Bark! Ticket filed: T-xxx'). This provides a human-in-the-loop feedback method at the point of encounter without requiring the user to know the queue tooling.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (chat route handler), possibly a shared command parser utility
**Design rules:** OOP-first; no-sqlite; log every /ticket command (interface crossing)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: /ticket <description> parsed in all device chat POST handlers; delegates to cc_queue.py add; returns T-id in response. Out of scope: /sprint, /close or other slash commands (future), per-device ticket tagging beyond device name.
**Completion criteria:** (1) POST to any device chat with message '/ticket some problem description' creates a queue entry and returns the T-id; (2) ticket is tagged with the device name; (3) non-/ticket messages unaffected; (4) tests: /ticket command creates entry; response contains T-id; regular message unaffected
